Nuestro desafío arraiga  en la búsqueda de aquellos conceptos fundantes e inherentes a los proyectos y en la sinergia de los mismos. Mediante la integración del arte y el diseño como elementos transformadores y diferenciadores, se logra la integración entre partes indiferenciadas, acción que genera energía y armonía en las cosas. 

"Para encontrarte en lo infinito 
has de diferenciar para luego juntar." Goethe

Incorporating art and design -as transformer and differential elements- we achieve the integration between non differentiated parts, action that generates both energy and harmony in things.

"To find yourself in the infinite
you must differentiate to gather afterwards". Goethe

This is our focus, our target, our modus operandi. Our way to generate surplus and bring authenticity and genuineness in the things that we impulse.

Short Description:
Mattelsa is a premium streetwear brand based in Colombia, dedicated to creating a community that values life, sustainability, and high-quality clothing. The brand originated from an underground retail concept and has evolved to focus on quality and respect for both people and the planet. With 49 stores across Colombia and a presence in 27 countries, Mattelsa aims to connect with streetwear enthusiasts who appreciate ethical fashion.

The company specializes in premium streetwear made from high-quality, natural-origin materials. Its product offerings include oversized men's clothing, women's tops, jeans, and essential streetwear staples like t-shirts and hoodies. Mattelsa emphasizes durability in its designs to promote responsible consumption. Through its Regeneration Culture foundation, the brand reinvests 100% of its profits into projects that support environmental recovery and education, reflecting its commitment to sustainability and community development.

Billabong Argentina is a subsidiary of Billabong International Limited, an Australian company known for its surf and snowboard apparel and accessories. Established in 1973, Billabong Argentina operates within the clothing and apparel retail industry, employing around 282 people and generating annual revenue of $9.9 million. The company is a key player in the regional market, contributing to Billabong's presence in over 25 countries.

The company offers a wide range of surf and snowboard clothing and accessories for men, women, and children. Their product lineup includes board shorts, neoprene wetsuits, snowboard clothing, fashion tops, and various accessories like watches and backpacks. Billabong Argentina also features an online e-commerce platform, providing customers with flexible payment options. The brand is dedicated to quality and durability, appealing to surfers, snowboarders, and lifestyle consumers who appreciate both technical performance and fashion in action sports culture.

Como consecuencia de la gran competencia frente los mercados textiles  mundiales, el sector textil chileno se ha visto afectado generado una importante vacío en la oferta.

Desde Sago nace la necesidad de generar un proyecto creativo que dinamice el sector con una propuesta de diseño desde una perspectiva de producción local con un fuerte énfasis en la innovación.
SAGO Diseña y desarrolla proyectos textiles a través de los estampados, estos abarcan diversas técnicas.

Desde Sago Estudio podemos trabajar proyectos de diseño personalizados adaptándonos diferentes desafíos.

Desde sago estudio realizamos diversos talleres sobre técnicas de estampados y trabajo textil, que abordan desde el gráfico de moda hasta la manufactura, tanto tradicionales como experimentales. Taller de colorido, tinturas, serigrafía o búsqueda de tendencias son algunos de los planteamientos para los cursos a desarrollar.

Short Description:
LA MARCA


AMPI VERA es una marca de moda contemporánea fundada en Buenos Aires, Argentina en el 2016. La marca se centra en la creación de prendas únicas y atemporales que fusionan diseño y calidad. El color es el sentido de todas sus colecciones. AMPI VERA es conocida por su estilo distintivo y su enfoque en la experimentación con colores y estampas. En cada colección se construye con una narrativa sólida que se basa en un concepto y un universo visual inspirado en el arte, el diseño y la creatividad.

AMPI VERA ofrece una variedad de prendas, desde básicos hasta abrigos y sastrería, todos caracterizados por su estilo único y atemporal.


PROPÓSITO


Como diseñadora de indumentaria, siento la necesidad de crear prendas que comuniquen, que tengan su propio lenguaje y una identidad visual definida. Lo más importante es que estas prendas inspiren a las personas a ser la mejor versión de sí mismas.

La esencia de la marca radica en potenciar la libertad, la audacia y la expresividad a través del indumento. A través de cada colección AMPI VERA invita a su comunidad a vivir con autenticidad y a expresarse sin restricciones.

Short Description:
Founded in 1938, Arlei is currently one of the largest exporters of crust leather in the world. The company began as a small vegetable sole leather trading house distributing goods to local footwear and handbag manufacturers, and gradually expanded with the construction of a vegetable tannery in Salta (northwestern Argentina) and a chrome/chrome free tannery in Santa Fe (northeastern Argentina), and later on its finishing plant and a cutting and sewing facility in Buenos Aires. Initially under the management of  its founder Mr. Arturo Leiser, and now continued by the following generation, Viviana and Alan Leiser, leading over 2000 employees, the company is acknowledged as one of the leading tanneries in South America.
The company's leather processing facilities use state of the art technology to process over nine thousand hides a day, 7 days a week, consuming around 22% of the total slaughter of Argentina hides, exporting a wide range of products to all five continents. Arlei is proud to be the first Argentine tannery to have certified
ISO14001, ISO 9001 y TS 16949.
The company complies with the strictest quality standards required by the industry leaders including automobile and furniture manufacturers worldwide

Established in Colombia in 1993, The Color Wear is a fashion company dedicated to the design, manufacture, and retail of women's clothing.

With stores in 12 different cities across the country, The Color Wear has garnered a strong national presence in the Colombian market and established itself as an industry leader. In 2018, the brand opened itself to the international market, and has ever since been shipping internationally to various locations across North, Central, and South America.

These materials are used in the creation of casual and formal pieces for modern and stylish women who appreciate fashion, yet value the durability of a high-quality product.

The Color Wear shines for its unique approach to fashion: always maintaining a love for classics without sacrificing novelty and color. This creative way of interpreting fashion design, coupled with the unmatched quality of a 100% Colombian product, has allowed the brand to endure over time and become a symbol of style at a national and international level.

Short Description:
MAYDI is a contemporary and bespoke knitwear brand based in Argentina.
The brand was created in 2014, upon my return to Argentina, expressing my Franco-Argentine culture. The first question I asked myself when creating the brand was: what are the main resources that can be found in Argentina to create a brand that is 100% local?
I started investigating and discovered that Argentina is one of the main exporters of merino wool and other natural fibers. From the first moment, my purpose was to create sustainable products with a unique contemporary design. That is why, at MAYDI, we don't have stock; each piece is entirely handmade, using local raw materials. We open the doors to the world, showing the value of our garment and promoting handmade pieces crafted using traditional and autochthonous techniques. Along the way, I have had the pleasure of working with marvelous artisan women and weaver men. They have always worked from home since the beginning.
MAYDI uses only natural fibers and organic yarns in our hand-woven fabrics.
The timeless spirit of each piece manifests itself in a 100% contemporary and refined design, carried out through ancient techniques of hand-knitting, manual weaving-loom, and crochet. 
MAYDI, the Wildlife Friendly Enterprise Network (WFEN), and the Wildlife Conservation Society-Argentina (WCS) are pleased to announce the debut of Certified Wildlife Friendly™ fibers from Patagonia Argentina at Paris Fashion Week. Using luxurious merino wool produced by the "Patagonian Fibers with a Conscience" program from a sustainable rancher's cooperative called "Merino de Peninsula Valdés™" Wildlife Friendly™, the Merino Peninsula Valdés is based on the coexistence between ovine breeding for wool production purposes and the healthy population of wildlife.
MAYDI sets the quality bar very high, ensuring meticulous attention to detail in the entire creation process of each garment, handmade from start to finish.

Short Description:
Caffarena is a Chilean company with a rich history dating back to 1888, when Blas Caffarena Chiozza arrived in Iquique. The company expanded to Santiago in 1920 and established its main operations in Maipú in 1969. By its 90th anniversary, Caffarena was being led by the fourth generation of the family.

Caffarena specializes in intimate apparel and clothing, with key products including panties, chemisettes, leggings, t-shirts, and swimwear. The company has a strong market presence in Chile, where its panties dominated 65% of the market by the late 20th century. Caffarena has also expanded internationally, with products featured in Victoria's Secret catalog in the United States and distribution in countries like Peru, Argentina, Uruguay, and Mexico. The first direct international store opened in Lima, Peru in 2019, and the company celebrated its 100th anniversary in 2020.

Short Description:
SAKE is a living laboratory where ancestral techniques, biomaterials, and ecosystems converge to redefine design and fashion. Founded after more than a decade of immersive research and collaboration across the Andes and Amazon, SAKE bridges ancestral intelligence with contemporary innovation to create fashion that is alive, reciprocal, and regenerative.

Our mission is clear: preserve and revitalize ancestral knowledge; develop biomaterials that extend the lifespan of ecosystems; strengthen community economies through fair, participatory, and regenerative models; and offer alternatives to extractive fashion by creating materials that embody life, territory, and memory.

Governance at SAKE is rooted in accountability, traceability, and reciprocity. For three consecutive years, we have been Fair Trade Certified, ensuring equitable pay, safe working conditions, and compliance with international labor standards. Production timelines, pricing, and innovation are co-defined with artisans and associations, guaranteeing both transparency and financial sovereignty. Growth follows ecological rhythms and cultural protocols, never exceeding what the land and people can sustain.

Each of our materials is developed in dialogue with territory and community: hand-spun alpaca and natural dyes with Pinchimuro in Cusco; native cotton and palm fibers with Shunku Warmi Kuna in San Martín; wild rubber with Eco Bosques Chazuta; paiche fish leather in Ucayali; and biodegradable vegetable-tanned hides with our Lima lab.

Our sustainability in practice engages more than 60 artisans, Indigenous leaders, and forest guardians. Programs such as the Caravana Andina support food security and agricultural sovereignty in the Andes, while Amazkin protects over 240 hectares of Amazon forest. Circular investments extend to greenhouses, clean water systems, health, and education.

We are a bridge between ancestral and contemporary worlds, proving that fashion can regenerate rather than extract.

Creada en 2013 e influenciados por el movimiento Skateboarding de principios del 2000, inspirados por la música underground en sus diferentes expresiones; Body Moving (Baile), DJ & mucho sabor. Nacidos con el ADN Latino, pero con una fuerte influencia en la cultura Street de Los Ángeles, con unos guiños a la moda Asiática & adaptaciones al más puro estilo minimalista, DYNAMO es la Rebel Crew que busca ser disruptiva y convertirse en referente para la moda streetwear actual, democratizándola y marcando la diferencia por medio de siluetas, tendencias & diseños con gráficas desde los básicos más puros y elementales hasta el color y sabor propio de nuestra ubicación geográfica, pasando por un futurismo tecnológico, & valiéndonos de diferentes técnicas de estampación y procesos en telas e índigos que nos permitan expresar toda nuestra esencia.

DYNAMO buscamos romper los estándares establecidos, apelamos a la irreverencia y le damos voz a los que representamos. Decimos con nuestras creaciones Io que muchos no se atreven a decir.

DYNAMO BRAND inicia en el año 2013 con el canal de ventas mayorista, cuyo objetivo en su momento fue suplir la demanda creciente de moda streetwear.

Pepa Pombo is a Colombian ready-to-wear fashion brand that specializes in knitwear. Founded in 1978 by designer Pepa Pombo, the brand is now led by her daughter, Mónica Holguín, as Creative Director. Pepa Pombo quickly gained recognition in the 1980s, becoming one of the leading fashion names in Colombia and Mexico. The brand is known for its unique heritage, featuring intricate knitwear designs, innovative techniques, and exclusive fabrics.

The product line includes a variety of ready-to-wear knitwear, such as dresses, outerwear, and vibrant printed pieces with innovative textures. Recent collections, like SS25 "La Bohème - D2," focus on empowering women's fashion with bold, bohemian styles. Pepa Pombo continues to adapt its legacy for new generations, offering garments that emphasize distinctiveness, multi-functionality, and versatility. The brand also participates in events like Bogotá Fashion Week, showcasing its commitment to the fashion industry.

Short Description:
Dunkelvolk is a Peruvian urban lifestyle clothing brand established in 1996 by three inline skating enthusiasts. Initially focused on creating wide-leg jeans for skaters, the brand has evolved to cater to a broader audience, including bikers and surfers. The name "Dunkelvolk," meaning "Dark People" in German, reflects the brand's passion and adventurous spirit.

The company offers a variety of men's and unisex apparel and accessories designed for urban and active lifestyles. Key product categories include jeans, caps, outerwear, and practical accessories like wallets and backpacks. Dunkelvolk frequently features sales and discounts, making its products accessible to a wide range of customers. The brand is known for its durable designs, suitable for action sports, and is sold primarily through its official online store.

With a commitment to customer experience, TRIATHLON SPORT continuously improves its stores and services.

Short Description:
Future Visions es la primera cadena de Streetwear y Lifestyle del Perú. Tenemos como metas principales la creación de una experiencia única de compra; desarrollar contenido informativo, inspirador e innovador para nuestras Redes Sociales y  Proporcionar un canal de ventas nuevo para que marcas internacionales puedan acercar sus productos mas exclusivos y especiales a consumidores locales. 
El sueño de Future Visions comenzó en 2016. Durante estos ultimos años, hemos viajado a las ciudades mas referentes de la comunidad streetwear mundial, visitando tiendas, asistiendo a eventos y ferias, y participando en la cultura de la moda internacional. En esta serie de viajes abrimos los caminos y encontramos la inspiración para crear la primera boutique de lifesyle y moda del Perú.
En 2018 iniciamos relaciones comerciales con prestigiosas marcas a nivel mundial y diseñamos la forma en la que traeríamos la cultura streetwear a nuestra comunidad.
Hoy, desde nuestra tienda online, exponemos y vendemos una variedad de productos premium y vanguardia, seleccionados y curados directamente por nuestro equipo. Muy pronto, contaremos también con nuestro showroom privado y con una boutique que será nuestra Flagship Store.
Nuestra propuesta pasa por redifinir el concepto de la moda en el Perú y en generar una comunidad que no solo se nutre de artículos de diseño, sino que vive su vida a través de ellos y expresa su cultura en las diferente plataformas de comunicación. Por eso, Future Visions es, además de una tienda de moda, una ventana de creación de contenido en donde se comparte el estilo de vida, la pasión y la perseverancia de nuestra marca.

The company focuses on providing technology-driven banking and financial services to small and medium-sized enterprises (SMEs) across Mexico, Colombia, Peru, and the U.S. Kapital achieved unicorn status with a valuation of $1.3 billion following a $100 million Series C funding round. It operates as a publicly traded entity and has reported revenue of $257.8 million.

Kapital offers a comprehensive ecosystem for business finance management through online and mobile platforms. Its services include business checking accounts, loans, credit options, payment automation, and investment management. The company utilizes proprietary AI tools for sales projections and operational insights, enhancing its offerings for SMEs. With a growing customer base of nearly 300,000, Kapital is recognized as a top innovator in the financial sector, emphasizing next-generation compliance and efficient capital access for underserved businesses.

Short Description:
Bowen nace en 1995 como un nuevo concepto en indumentaria para el hombre urbano. El uso de diferentes materiales y texturas lograron una perfecta combinación entre estilo y comodidad y fueron los pilares de sus creaciones desde el primer momento.

Inspirados en la música y las calles londinenses, se pensó cómo lograr prendas que se adapten el estilo de vida urbano actual. El uso de materiales genuinos como el cuero, dénim y el algodón pima se combinaron con un diseño de vanguardia, logrando colecciones versátiles, confortables y llenas de detalles que marcan la diferencia.

En la actualidad BOWEN se ha convertido en un referente de estilo, logrando convertir el "casual wear" en una alternativa ideal para la vida en la ciudad.

Short Description:
Kaya Unite is a Chilean apparel brand based in the Santiago Metropolitan Region. The company promotes a positive lifestyle focused on outdoor sports, nature conservation, and African-rooted music and culture. Founded with a commitment to high-quality, original clothing designs, Kaya Unite emphasizes eco-friendliness and sustainable practices. It operates physical stores in Chile and has expanded to locations like Seattle, engaging with environmentally conscious communities.

Kaya Unite specializes in wholesale and retail apparel inspired by nature and outdoor adventures. Its product offerings include bottles, vests, polar fleece, beanies, and pants, all designed to support an active, nature-connected lifestyle. The brand is certified as a B Corporation, reflecting its dedication to positive environmental and community impact. Kaya Unite uses 100% recyclable materials and donates profits to provide clothing and sports equipment to those in need. The company employs around 58-63 people and maintains strong relationships with its collaborators and users.

WAPS™ is an Argentine company that designs and manufactures stylish and comfortable footwear for everyday use. The brand focuses on creating shoes that blend elegance with all-day comfort, catering to women who need versatile options for various occasions, including work, travel, and social events.

The core product line features shoes made with exclusive knitting technology, allowing the upper part to adapt flexibly to the foot's shape. WAPS™ shoes include a flexible sole, cushioned insole, and breathable inner lining that absorbs sweat, ensuring durability and comfort. The seamless design prevents irritation, and the shoes are machine washable for easy maintenance. Popular models include Model Lisa, Model One, Model Zen, and several others, with some designated as bestsellers or new releases. WAPS™ also offers free shipping across Argentina and accepts all major credit cards, enhancing the customer experience.

REITE is a Chilean startup founded by four engineers from the University of Chile. The company specializes in Mini Tiendas Autónomas, or autonomous mini-stores, which utilize IoT, hardware, artificial intelligence, and computer vision. Short Description:
Mycorium Biotech is an Argentine startup founded in 2022 in Rosario, Santa Fe. The company specializes in sustainable biomaterials, focusing on mycelium-based bioleather produced through precision fermentation and synthetic biology using agro-industrial waste. Mycorium develops circular and scalable biomaterials as alternatives to traditional animal leather and synthetic plastics, addressing environmental challenges in the fashion industry.

The core product, Mycorium™ bioleather, is a biodegradable material made from fungal mycelium. It is soft, strong, and breathable, produced without the use of animals or plastics. The company employs a proprietary process that includes solid-state fermentation of agro-industrial waste, followed by post-treatments to enhance durability and performance. Mycorium's applications currently include handbags, accessories, and phone cases, with plans to expand into footwear, apparel, and automotive trims.

Mycorium Biotech offers B2B sustainable material solutions for various industries, including fashion, manufacturing, and automotive. The company has garnered interest from international brands like Adidas and is actively seeking partnerships and funding to scale its operations.

¿POR QUÉ ELEGIR BRIGANTI PARA TRABAJAR?

IMPACTO
Nuestro éxito se basa en nuestra capacidad para convertir las aportaciones en impacto. Eso significa que respaldamos las ideas, vengan de donde vengan. Al desafiarnos a aprender y crecer todos los días, creamos un entorno que antepone la innovación al ego y permite a todos dejar su huella.

EQUIPO DINÁMICO
El trabajo en Briganti ocurre en equipos pequeños y dinámicos. La apertura y el intercambio de ideas no solo se fomenta, se vive. La colaboración se extiende más allá de tu equipo inmediato, ya que nos unimos a través de funciones, ubicaciones y niveles de gestión para lograr objetivos compartidos.

DIRIGE EL CAMBIO
En Briganti nuestras ideas son grandes y nuestras acciones son audaces. Cada rol tiene un impacto. Por eso buscamos personas como vos, personas con ganas de crecer personal y profesionalmente. Dia a dia nos comprometemos con tu desarrollo no solo profesional sino también cultural, social y emocional.

BERGER JOYEROS 

Berger aspira a ser el mejor distribuidor de joyería/relojería de lujo en el mundo.

Tras un siglo de andadura, Berger se ha convertido en un emblema de elegancia. 

El inicio de Berger se remite a principios del siglo veinte cuando el Sr. Alex Elías Berger – nacido en Polonia en 1895- fundó en Amsterdam, Holanda, un taller dedicado al tallado de diamantes creando una pequeña industria del rubro. Más tarde, en 1918 al termino del la Primera Guerra Mundial el joyero se trasladó a Amberes, Bélgica, en donde comenzó de nuevo con un tallado de diamantes consolidándose como uno de los mejores joyeros en la industria europea.

En Marzo de 1943 la familia Berger emigra a la Ciudad de México en donde el Sr. Maurice y Sylvain Berger, segunda generación, dieron continuación al negocio en un esquema de crecimiento y desarrollo paulatino que comenzó con una oficina en la calle de Madero en 1943.

En los años 90 se incorpora la tercera generación, Ari y Sergio Berger, y con ella la idea de expandir el negocio a las boutiques. Durante esta expansión, se incluyeron marcas de Alta Relojería como Rolex, Patek Philippe, Audemars Piguet, A. Lange & Söhne, Vacheron Constantin, Panerai y Hublot, entre otras.

 

Actualmente cuentan con las boutiques de Masaryk, Antara Polanco, Paseo Interlomas y Vía Santa Fe. En ellas los clientes pueden encontrar finas piezas elaboradas con diamantes y piedras preciosas; así como las mejores marcas a nivel mundial de alta relojería.

Calle 12 #16, en Tlanepantla, Edo. De México, que es filial de TELAS LUA S.A. de C.V.
El 2 de mayo del 2005, se constituye Especialización Textil S.A. de C.V. y en enero del 2006 se
traslada al domicilio actual, que es Av. Revolución 570 Tlaquepaque Jalisco. A través de estos años de
éxito y con la visión de brindar productos con valor agregado y especializada en ropa para dama se
funda Moda Club en 2006.

Recognized as an international market leader, OndadeMar has been featured in prominent magazines such as Vogue, Bazaar, and InStyle, and is ranked among the top swimwear brands by Elle and Forbes. Capittana is a Peruvian swimwear brand founded in 2014 by siblings Raffaella and Stefano Porcari. Based in Barranco, Lima, the company has grown to become a leading name in swimwear, with an estimated annual revenue of $5.7M to $7.4M and a team of 17-31 employees. The brand's name, meaning "female captain," reflects its mission to empower strong and independent women.

Capittana specializes in luxury swimwear, activewear, and beachwear, featuring handmade designs that incorporate traditional Peruvian artistry. Their product range includes bikinis, one-pieces, dresses, cover-ups, and accessories, all crafted with a focus on sustainability and eco-friendliness. The company emphasizes artisanal craftsmanship, partnering with at-risk women and single mothers in Lima to create a stable income and safe working environments. Capittana operates a physical store in Miami and sells its products through e-commerce channels, ensuring global reach and accessibility.

Short Description:
Desde nuestros inicios en 1998, somos una marca de streetwear que se ha mantenido fiel a una filosofía y un objetivo: resaltar la personalidad acompañando el estilo de vida de un público que se anima a más, que busca prendas cómodas, diferenciadas, y descontracturadas. 

 

Con una trayectoria de más de 20 años en el mercado, la experiencia nos ha permitido y también exigido complementar nuestra propuesta, sumando las marcas Kristobelga y 70B-Lab, cada una con una identidad y público marcados y enfocados en cubrir todas las necesidades de nuestros clientes que crecen junto con nosotros.

 

Con nuestra marca insignia North Sails N+, proponemos nuestra visión del streetwear masculino y femenino, adaptando la tendencia urbana  de cada temporada a las necesidades de nuestros seguidores fieles. 

Kristobelga canaliza más específicamente nuestro espíritu descontracturado y más under, el mundo del surf y el skate combinados en un lifestyle de playa y pavimento cool.

70B-Lab completa la propuesta con prendas más clásicas y elegantes, para un público más maduro y selecto que pone atención a los detalles.

 

Nuestro equipo de diseño, en constante investigación de nuevas tendencias, identifica y desarrolla colecciones diferenciadas para cada marca, actualizando a nuestro público, siendo fiel al sello que nos caracteriza. 

 

Actualmente contamos con dos locales y una franquicia en los principales centros comerciales de Montevideo, sumando la venta al por mayor para todo el país.

Short Description:
Founded in 2016 in Barranquilla, Colombia, Waimari is a luxury resort wear brand known for timeless elegance, artisanal craft, and ethical purpose.

Crafted for women who seek sophistication in every escape, Waimari designs elevated resort essentials, fresh dresses with intricate detailing, statement separates, and romantic silhouettes, perfect for destination weddings, coastal retreats, and unforgettable summer moments.

Each piece is handmade in Colombia by local artisans, many of whom are women and single mothers, preserving heritage techniques while empowering communities. The brand proudly embraces sustainability through small-batch production, quality over quantity, and a deep respect for the people behind each garment.

Waimari has captured the attention of the global fashion industry, with features in Vogue, Elle, Harper's Bazaar, Marie Claire, and Condé Nast Traveler. The brand's signature pieces have been highlighted in "The White Lotus" tv show, celebrated for their romantic silhouettes and effortless luxury. Whether showcased on runways like Colombiamoda or spotted in fashion capitals, Waimari continues to redefine resort wear, not just as a seasonal wardrobe, but as a celebration of meaningful design and modern femininity.

Founders:

Waimari was founded by Colombian sisters Stephanie Martínez and María Catalina Martínez, inspired by a desire to honor their family's legacy of craftsmanship while redefining resort wear through a lens of modern sophistication.

Stephanie, with a background in fashion marketing from Parsons and experience at global luxury houses like Gucci, Cartier, and Michael Kors, leads the brand's strategic vision and global growth. María Catalina, trained in design at institutions such as Marangoni, FIT, and Parsons, shapes the creative direction with an eye for timeless beauty, femininity, and artisanal detail.

Short Description:
Founded in Cartagena, Colombia, in 1993, Beatriz Camacho stems from a genuine appreciation for comfort and subtle sophistication - poetic and sincere. 

With acute, honest attention to material quality and detail, every garment looks to celebrate individuality through effortless, conscious design, thus balancing the creative essence of an artist with the pragmatism of the contemporary woman. It is under this emotional yet innovative approach that Beatriz continues to imagine timeless pieces rooted on a fervent passion for nature, art, and culture.

With roots in Colombia's Caribbean coast, and more specifically, Cartagena, the sea has been a constant pillar in every collection. Featuring exploratory prints, earthy colors, and often asymmetrical silhouettes, all pieces tend to also portray a cultural legacy. Even more so, Beatriz believes in the importance of having a special connection to each garment. This is why she focuses on creating clothes that last longer, look good, and feel even better on the skin. The use of natural fabrics such as linen, silk, and cotton are and have always been essential to the brand.

Short Description:
Battassi Design Studio is an Italo-Mexican design firm based in León, Mexico, founded by Alberto Tassinari and Francisco Javier Batta Martinez. The studio specializes in the design and development of footwear, bags, and leather accessories, bringing over 15 years of experience to the industry. Battassi emphasizes innovation, artisanal excellence, and sustainability, holding a Leather Working Group certification.

The studio offers a range of services, including conceptualization, material selection, and detailed technical documentation for manufacturing. They oversee the full production process, with a capacity of about 200 pairs of footwear per month, scalable based on demand. Battassi also provides educational content through online courses, such as "Design Shoes from Scratch," which covers the fundamentals of footwear design.

In addition to custom designs for client brands, Battassi has developed its own footwear line, SISTA, known for its conscious and innovative approach. The studio has collaborated with over 80 fashion brands across various regions, including Mexico, the United States, Europe, and Asia.

Short Description:
Kiabi UY is the Uruguayan branch of the French fashion retailer Kiabi, which opened its first store in Latin America in 2023. The company focuses on providing affordable, family-oriented clothing and accessories. Founded in 1978, Kiabi has grown to operate 560 stores across 23 countries, serving 23 million customers globally. In 2022, it achieved a turnover of 2.2 billion euros.

The Kiabi UY store, located at Car One, spans 1,100 m² and represents a US$2 million investment. The local team consists of about 14 employees, with a commitment to ethical fashion and sustainability through initiatives like KIABI ACTS FOR A BETTER WORLD. Kiabi UY offers a range of ready-to-wear apparel designed by a team of 56 in-house stylists, with a focus on responsible and inclusive fashion. The brand supports both physical retail and e-commerce, aiming to blend local and global reach under the slogan "Siempre más para tu familia" (Always more for your family).

Grupo Basic S.A. is a prominent textile manufacturing company in Paraguay, part of the Texcin Group. It focuses on producing apparel and related products under the BASIC and UNICO brands, aiming to make fashion accessible in the local market. Established in 2014, Grupo Basic contributes to Texcin's mission of creating a productive and industrial center for high-quality fashion products, emphasizing nearshoring and rapid delivery.

The company plays a significant role in Paraguay's textile sector, which exported $258.2 million in 2021. Grupo Basic supports this industry by employing nearly 9,000 people across 57 maquila companies. It targets local consumers while also serving international fashion and retail leaders through strategic partnerships. Additionally, Grupo Basic is committed to social and environmental responsibility, integrating these values into its operations.

Short Description:
Fundada el 1 de Marzo de 1951, la AICP - Asociación Industrial de Confeccionistas del Paraguay, es una de las más antiguas y prestigiosas Cámaras Gremiales de Paraguay. 

Su misión principal consiste en promover la industria confeccionista paraguaya en todos sus aspectos, desarrollo tecnológico, capacitación y productividad. 

La AICP representa los intereses del sector confeccionista ante los organismos gubernamentales paraguayos y las entidades del exterior. 

Las industrias confeccionistas aglutinadas en la AICP contribuyen con más de un 60% de la vestimenta producida en Paraguay y, en 2001, representaron un 90% del total de la indumentaria exportada.

Short Description:
Pietà is a fashion label born in the prisons of Lima, Latin America.

An impertinent and irreverent project, independent and spontaneous. Far from the marketing diktat, it offers an alternate conception of fashion. This project, influenced by the counter-cultures, refuses to submit to the social norms of a society devoted to the mass consumption. We do menswear. Sometimes goods for women too. The important is to make cool stuff.

The project is born in a prison of Lima and successfully developed in others prisons. The collections are entirely produced in prisons without any external help.  Even our models come from here. In this way it allows inmates, often left to their own devices, to independently generate an income and develop their skills allowing for a faster rehabilitation. Each day of work decreases the prisoner's sentence by a full day. 

All the Pietà clothes are created in the fashioning, knitting, and leather workshops of the different prisons where the prisoners produce, with passion and pride, garments that will travel around the world. Every piece is unique, numbered, made in limited edition, and signed by the craftsman who made it. 

Pietà uses natural, ecological and recycled materials, working the most noble and rarest fibers on Earth such as alpaca, cashmere, pima cotton or silk. Garments are composed of simple and minimalist cuts, from hard life inspiration for everyday life, with a story emerging behind every piece. 

This project was named Pietà after the chef d'oeuvre of Michelangelo. A piece depicting a difficult scene of accepting the Divine will, without any lamentation or pain. It is about sobriety and dignity. 

This is the state of mind of Pietà: in spite of our difficulties and prejudice, we don't give up. We demonstrate good work, humility and simplicity while being full of hope for the future. Finally the Pietà is the last step before the Resurrection, and a new rebirth.

Short Description:
VERDI is a Colombian textile studio founded in 2010 by siblings Tomás and Cristina Vera. The studio honors their late father, Carlos Vera Dieppa, a pioneer in combining natural fibers with metal threads. Under Tomás's creative direction, VERDI has become an international reference for artisanal luxury, known for its handwoven luxury goods that celebrate Colombia's cultural heritage.

The studio offers a diverse range of handwoven products, including homeware items like rugs and carpets, fashion accessories such as handbags, and conceptual art pieces. VERDI's unique craftsmanship intertwines natural fibers with metallic threads, using materials like fique fiber, cumare, and baby alpaca, all sourced from Latin America. Each piece is handwoven by female artisans in Colombia, showcasing the country's traditional craftsmanship. VERDI has received notable recognition, including winning the Artisanal Project of the Year at the Latin American Fashion Awards and being the first Colombian studio to exhibit solo at Salone del Mobile for three consecutive years.

Vicuña Corp. is a Canadian mining company established as a 50/50 joint venture between BHP and Lundin Mining. The company is dedicated to developing a significant copper district in the Vicuña District, located on the Argentina-Chile border. Vicuña focuses on exploring and developing major deposits of copper, gold, and silver, with the goal of creating a large-scale mining complex that emphasizes sustainable practices and shared infrastructure.

The company operates advanced projects such as the Josemaría deposit in San Juan Province, Argentina, which is planned as a conventional open-pit mine, and the Filo del Sol project, which spans both Argentina and Chile. These projects are designed to optimize costs and support staged expansions, positioning Vicuña among the world's largest resources of copper, gold, and silver. Vicuña Corp. is committed to workforce safety, environmental management, and community relations as it advances its initiatives.

Yanbal operates a direct sales model where independent Style Advisors sell products and build teams, benefiting from digital tools and resources to enhance their business. This model supports flexible, entrepreneurial growth, allowing advisors to manage their own sales and earnings.

Short Description:
'-Fundadora & Diseñadora-
Alexandra Jiménez, Diseñadora de Modas, Comunicadora y Bailarina peruana que creció en Los Ángeles, California – influenciada desde una temprana edad por el arte, música y estilo urbano.

Al graduarse de la carrera de Comunicaciones de la Universidad de Lima, inició sus estudios de moda en Buenos Aires, Argentina para luego perfeccionarse en The Fashion Institute of Design & Merchandising en Los Ángeles, California.

Es en esta ciudad donde se desenvolvió como Diseñadora de Modas, Fashion Stylist & Asesora para bailarines, coreógrafos, músicos y empresas del rubro. Fue responsable del área de Vestuario en un canal internacional de televisión y participó como Coordinadora en el backstage de diferentes marcas de Diseñadores de Moda en Los Angeles Fashion Week 2012.

Para el año 2014 se reubica en Lima – Perú donde expande su experiencia como Asesora de Imagen, Personal Shopper y Maquilladora Profesional. Además, obtiene el cargo de Docente y Asesora para diferentes institutos de moda y diseño, así como para entidades importantes como InterCorp. Todo esto mientras asistía a sus clases de danza a lo largo de su vida personal y profesional.

El mismo año se convierte en Fundadora de Kinetix, haciendo networking desde un inicio y creando alianzas con academias de danza, yoga, pilates, entre otras dedicadas al bienestar físico y mental.

Kinetix tiene como misión que, mujeres apasionadas y con metas claras no sólo se vistan con la indumentaria adecuada y original según las actividades que realicen; sino que también se sientan empoderadas, líderes y capaces de lo que se propongan con toda determinación y una actitud positiva.

Los valores que la marca aporta es la practicidad, versatilidad y originalidad; dándonos la libertad de vestir ropa y accesorios deportivos para realizar diversas disciplinas o simplemente porque amamos el estilo y performance de los diseños únicos de Kinetix.

Short Description:
Shasa is a Mexican fast-fashion retailer founded in 1995 by brothers Armando and Carlo Dollero. The company specializes in women's apparel, accessories, jewelry, beauty products, and footwear. With over 100 stores primarily in Mexico and a growing online presence, Shasa is headquartered in Lerma de Villada, Estado de México. It operates as a privately held, family-owned business and employs around 1,690 people directly.

Shasa focuses on delivering fresh daily fashion for women, offering a wide range of products including dresses, skirts, pants, jackets, and more. The company produces most of its items in Mexico and introduces 52 annual collections inspired by global trends. Shasa emphasizes a vertically integrated supply chain for quick market response and competitive pricing, while also incorporating sustainable practices. The company supports various causes, including donations to Casa Hogar Alegra since 2008.

PIER Systems, Inc., also known as PIEERS, is a communication technologies company founded in 2000 and based in Bellingham, Washington. The company specializes in on-demand, web-based software for crisis management and public information response. It was established by Gerald Baron, who drew from his experience in managing public communications during a major disaster. Key executives include CEO Kevin Boxx and Senior VP Gerald Baron.

The flagship product, The PIER System, is an internet software and services platform that supports information management, crisis communications, business continuity, disaster recovery, public relations, mass notification, news monitoring, and press release distribution. This tool centralizes communication during disasters, enhancing the efficiency of information distribution and response. PIER Systems serves notable clients in the energy, oil, and government sectors, including major companies like BP International, Shell, and Valero Energy Corporation, as well as various U.S. government organizations.

Short Description:
Real Women Brand on a mission to empower our community.
By Women For Women.

CIHUAH comes from the Nahuatl word for "women", recognizing and acknowledging the indigenous women who create traditional craft designs, their ancient knowledge, and culture. CIHUAH garments reflect the pattern of Indigenous clothing, respecting their basic geometry while creating a contemporary look. All pieces are a sophisticated vision in white and black, designed with obsessive attention to the straight line and perfection; an exercise of confrontation between the line and the human figure. Architectural theory and techniques are applied, on a small scale, to each garment, using: geometry, proportion, aesthetics, construction-deconstruction, symmetry-asymmetry, balance-imbalance.

AWARDS AND RECOGNITION
- 2013: Published in the Directory of Mexican Design presented in the MOMA of New York.
- October 2013: Vanessa Guckel receives the Quorum Award in the Fashion Design Category.
- Semifinalist in the contest Vogue Who's On Next Mexico 2013, 2014 and 2016.
- July 2015: PopUp Store in Paris.
- December 2015: Vanessa receives a recognition from Lancôme for the creation and success of her brand in the fashion industry in Mexico.
- February 2016: the brand presents an exclusive piece during MACO.
- April 2016: Vanessa Guckel was considered among the 100 Women Leaders of Mexico by The Universal newspaper.
- Cihuah presents its collections in Mercedez Benz Fashion Week Mexico.
- Cihuah invites its best clients to the Maison Lancôme Mexico.
- Cihuah celebrates the Day of France in the presidential suite of the Sheraton Maria Isabel.
- April 2017: CIHUAH presents FW17 in MBFWMX with a Michael Nyman performance.
- Best Fashion Runway 2017 in the Mexico´s Fashion Digital Awards.
- Cihuah is published in several fashion magazines such as Vogue Mexico, Elle Mexico, Harper's Bazaar Español, L'Officiel Mexico, Luxury Guide Magazine, etc.

Short Description:
Polybion is a Mexican biotechnology startup founded in 2014, specializing in sustainable biomaterials. The company develops Celium™, a vegan leather alternative made from bacterial cellulose, which is cultivated using agro-industrial fruit waste. With operations in Spain and a production facility in central Mexico, Polybion focuses on creating a scalable and regenerative material that mimics leather without harmful chemicals.

The company originated from a 2013 MIT Synthetic Biology competition and has evolved from its initial focus on mycelium materials to prioritize bacterial cellulose. Polybion's innovative approach has garnered several awards, including recognition in sustainable chemistry and textiles. Celium™ is customizable and versatile, targeting various sectors such as fashion, furniture, automotive, and cosmetics. The company aims to expand its production capacity and establish global partnerships while promoting responsible consumption and environmental sustainability.

Short Description:
En Aceros Progresivos Mexicanos ofrecemos soluciones en Manufactura Aditiva para los sectores automotriz, aeronáutico, metalmecánico, médico, quirúrgico, entre otros.

La Manufactura Aditiva (AM) o Impresión 3D, es un proceso de fabricación de piezas y modelos creados en computadora. Luego ese diseño pasa a una máquina especial de Manufactura Aditiva en metales. 
Su principio de fabricación "capa por capa", permite añadir material (polvo fino de metal) que será solidificado mediante un láser, únicamente donde sea necesario.

Por lo que podrás optimizar y mejorar el desempeño de tus componentes y modelos, reducir procesos de ensamblajes y etapas de mecanizado, además de acortar tiempos de producción y entrega, hacer diseños personalizados y a la medida, re diseñando lo existente; podrás crear prototipos rápidos y reducir peso en componentes... Con la Manufactura Aditiva, ¡la imaginación es el límite!

Creamos piezas funcionales en: Acero Inoxidable (15-5PH, 17-4PH, 316L, 420), Acero Maraging (18Ni300), Aluminio (AlSi10Mg), Cobalto Cromo Molibdeno Wolframio (CoCrMoW), Cobre (Cu), Bronce (Cu90Sn10), Inconel (625, 718), Titanio grado 5 (Ti64Al4v), entre otros metales.

Brindamos servicio a toda la República Mexicana

Lleva a cabo tus proyectos de la mano de Aceros Progresivos Mexicanos. Tenemos la confianza de decir que: "si se puede imaginar, se puede fabricar".

Tamaño volumétrico máximo de piezas es: 270 x 270 x 320 (mm)

¡Contáctanos para más información!

Chamela S.A.S is a Colombian women's fashion company founded in 1996 in Medellín. With over 27 years in the market, it operates in more than 20 countries and employs approximately 250-499 people. The company generates around $10M-$25M in revenue and is dedicated to enhancing women's self-esteem and style through its trendy apparel.

Chamela offers a diverse range of products across five main lines: swimwear, kids' clothing, workout apparel, lingerie, and protective clothing and shapewear. The designs focus on trendy styles, colors, and textures to create unique daily experiences. The company also engages in full package services for its shapewear line, covering everything from design to production.

In Colombia, Chamela has 26 stores and sells through various channels, including department stores, boutiques, and online platforms. Internationally, it has a presence in countries like the United States, Mexico, and Costa Rica, with physical stores in cities such as Guatemala City and Cancún. Chamela also supports the National Beauty Contest in Colombia, which selects the country's Miss Universe representative.

Ser líder en la producción, distribución y venta de productos de alta calidad, para satisfacer las necesidades de belleza y comodidad en el vestir de nuestros consumidores que buscan estar a la altura de la moda, brindando la total garantía en nuestros productos. 

VISIÓN
Ser una empresa líder, sólida y bien organizada con productos textiles de alta calidad de clase mundial.

Short Description:
Fracking Design is a sustainable fashion brand founded in Argentina by sisters Carla, Ornella, and Mora Basilotta. Established in 2018 or 2019, the company specializes in premium leather goods and accessories made from recycled plastic big bags sourced from the oil and gas fracking industry. Operating under a triple impact model, Fracking Design emphasizes environmental sustainability, social inclusion, and economic viability, and is certified as a B Corp.

The brand emerged from the sisters' previous experience in fashion, transitioning from traditional women's clothing to innovative, eco-friendly alternatives. They transform discarded sand bags into a viable textile material, producing items like backpacks and accessories. The production process includes disassembling and washing the bags, followed by industrial transformation and sewing in cooperatives. Fracking Design offers a lifetime guarantee on its products, allowing customers to return damaged items for free repairs. The brand aims to counter fast fashion through sustainable practices and non-verbal messaging.

FYERS Securities Private Limited is an Indian online stock trading and investment platform based in Bangalore. The company is dedicated to empowering traders and investors through advanced technology and low-cost brokerage services. It offers a comprehensive range of investment options, including equities, futures and options (F&O), commodities, mutual funds, exchange-traded funds (ETFs), and initial public offerings (IPOs).

The platform features an integrated ecosystem with web, app, and desktop solutions for seamless trading. Key offerings include zero account opening and maintenance fees, capped brokerage at ₹20 per order, and a Margin Trading Facility (MTF) with up to 4x leverage. FYERS also provides advanced analysis tools, such as the AI-Powered FYERS Intelligent Assistant for stock analysis and portfolio intelligence. With over 500,000 clients, FYERS is recognized for its commitment to customer satisfaction and has received multiple awards, including 'Best New Comer of the Year-2023' by TradingView.

* What is the market for our Metaverse Mall? *

Include all brands and retailers that want to adopt new technologies the innovate within an unprecedented experience for engagement, loyalty, and more revenue in 3D and immersive environments.

And include shoppers that want to buy from home in a realistic like in-store experience. 

Our technology has the potential to create millions of jobs and drive growth to any retail no matter the company's size.


* Why us? *

Because we are developing the tools and the environment to let shoppers and retailers interact like in-store from home.

The potential of our technology is huge. We don't know exactly the size of the market. We can say 1 billion, 1 trillion and maybe it will be not enough. What we know is that digital commerce as we know it today is ready to change to a new level, incorporating each day more 3D environments, 3D objects, and VR devices. The World is just starting new models of relationships between humans and devices. Today we use to be sit in front of a PC or looking down to read or touch our mobile.  But the near future will require new body movements to interact with computers, mobiles, and VR devices. This requires a giant amount of technology in terms of software and hardware.

Our company focuses on the development of software technologies for commercial interactions in 3D environments with VR devices, powered by iCloud Gaming. You can enjoy today the Metaverse Mall at www.go2virtual.shop

But given the current prevalence of cell phones and personal computers, we are obliged to develop intermediate phases, where 3D coexists with screens and immersive devices at the same time. So we consider not only VR devices but also PC and mobile for the development of our technologies.

Short Description:
Ecocitex is a Chilean company dedicated to textile recycling through a circular economy model. It transforms discarded clothing into 100% recycled wool, fabrics, home decor items, and apparel, all produced without using water or dyes. Founded to tackle textile waste, Ecocitex emphasizes reuse and recycling, employing women in labor reintegration programs as part of its operations.

The company offers a variety of products, including recycled wool on cones, home decor items like blankets, and custom corporate gifts. Ecocitex also provides a textile waste recycling program, allowing companies to collect and deliver clean textile waste while purchasing recycled products. This initiative supports waste reduction in Chile and promotes social impact through job creation and donations. Ecocitex has received several accolades, including the "Marca Chile" certification and the "Sustainable Brand of the Year" award at the 2024 Latin American Fashion Awards.

Sensation du Temps, founded in 1993, began in Punta del Este, Uruguay during the 2001 season.

It belongs to Pumak S.A., our family business founded in 1949, which took its first steps in the world of selective distribution as a diamond wholesaler for a small group of clients. We have two strategic points of sale, one located in the iconic Shopping Patio Bullrich in Buenos Aires and another at Enjoy Casino & Resort in Punta del Este. Nuestra Misión:
Fomentar la economía circular y el consumo responsable a través del reciclaje creativo y la reutilización de materiales. Creemos en la innovación como motor de cambio y en la colaboración como pilar para construir comunidades resilientes y conscientes.

Servicios para Empresas:

Workshops para RRHH y Bienestar Empresarial:

	•	Creatividad y Innovación: Talleres que fomentan el pensamiento creativo y la resolución de problemas utilizando materiales reciclados.
	•	Trabajo en Equipo: Actividades diseñadas para mejorar la cohesión del equipo y promover la colaboración.
	•	Conciencia Ambiental: Programas que educan sobre la sostenibilidad y la responsabilidad ambiental en el entorno laboral.

Activaciones de Marketing y Moda Sostenible:

	•	Experiencias Interactivas: Diseñamos activaciones que involucran a los participantes en el proceso de suprareciclaje, creando conciencia y generando impacto.
	•	Eventos y Exposiciones: Organización de eventos que destacan la importancia del reciclaje y la moda sostenible, atrayendo la atención del público y los medios.
	•	Consultoría de Sostenibilidad: Asesoramos a las empresas sobre cómo integrar prácticas sostenibles en sus operaciones y estrategias de marketing.

Proyectos Destacados:

•Trashumantes.org: Plataforma transmedia que promueve el suprareciclaje y conecta a artistas y comunidades para reducir desechos.
•Suprareciclaje.org: Directorio digital de diseño de upcycling con mejores prácticas y proyectos en Latinoamérica.
•Proyecto Puente: Capacitación en suprareciclaje para mujeres migrantes, mejorando su calidad de vida y brindándoles nuevas oportunidades laborales.

El estilo de Min Agostini refleja fuertemente su íntima relación con los textiles y la comprensión de la espacialidad. The design is shaped by each fabric's unique characteristics and constructed sculpturally into innovative forms.

Scappino is a luxury fashion house founded in 1914 in Turin, Italy, by Domenico Scappino. The brand specializes in high-quality silk products and apparel, combining traditional Italian craftsmanship with contemporary design. Initially recognized for silk garments and men's accessories, Scappino gained prominence across Europe shortly after World War I and was appointed the official supplier to the Royal House of Italy in the 1930s.

Today, Scappino operates as a fashion retailer and emporium, with a significant presence in Mexico, including headquarters in Guadalajara and operations in Mexico City. The company produces about 70% of its goods in owned facilities, ensuring high manufacturing standards. Scappino offers a diverse range of luxury apparel and accessories, including formal suits, casual shirts, shoes, watches, and fragrances, all crafted from premium textiles. The brand emphasizes quality, exclusivity, and refined Italian design, maintaining its legacy through generational expertise in fashion retail.

Short Description:
Nuestra marca Comienza un 4 de diciembre. Ese día marcó nuestra memoria, ya que abríamos las puertas de nuestro primer local.
Con emoción recordamos que se encontraba en Galería Libertad. Pleno centro de la ciudad de Rosario, Santa Fe.
Instantáneamente fuimos aceptados por el público.
En ese momento, el local vendía: ropa, carteras y zapatos. No teníamos muy en claro, qué rubro iba a ser el principal o definitivo. En ese entonces nos bautizamos como "The Bag & The Belt" Refiriéndonos exclusivamente a carteras y accesorios.
En el transcurso de esos primeros meses identificamos que el calzado era lo que más buscaban. Y sus preferidos eran los confeccionados por nosotros mismos.
A partir de ese momento crecimos y con ello la apertura de más locales.
Palace Garden, hoy situado en Córdoba 1617 Shopping del Siglo, Mitre 842, Portal Rosario Shopping, Alto Rosario Shopping, de la ciudad de Rosario.
Luego, nos fuimos haciendo lugar en otras provincias. Honduras 4802 (Caba), Alto Comauhe Shopping Neuquén, La Ribera Shopping Santa Fe Capital.
Varias franquicias en Reconquista, San Nicolás, Salta y Comodoro Rivadavia.
Felizmente anunciamos la última apertura en la provincia de Mendoza, Plaza Shopping.
En todo ese proceso de aperturas y crecimiento, la marca cambió a "The Bag Belt"
Encontramos en todos estos años, la capacidad de brindarle a nuestras clientas (mujeres diferentes y practicas) colecciones aceptadas año tras año.
Nos propusimos ofrecerles líneas con los más exquisitos gustos, con materiales importados, pero sin perder lo esencial; NUESTROS PROPIOS DISEÑOS!
Además no dejamos de apostar a nuestro país, todos nuestros cueros son regionales, de las mejores curtiembres que existen en la plaza.
Hoy, llegamos a los 21 años, luchados y exitosos. No dejamos de soñar y proyectar.
Con nuestra fuerza de trabajo, nuestra gente, la cual trabaja con profunda dedicación, para conseguir día a día que esta marca siga creciendo.

Short Description:
Makeno es un proyecto comercial construido alrededor del diseño, propone un estilo de vida en el que se conectan vestuario, joyería, complementos, objetos y mobiliario de hogar con aquellos consumidores que disfrutan del talento y les gusta diferenciarse.
Reafirmamos la importancia de facilitar un entorno creativo que acerque a los consumidores que aprecian el diseño y buscan un estilo de vida que los diferencie y les permita compartir experiencias únicas. 
Nuestro principal propósito consiste en conectar al diseñador y al consumidor final. En la tienda se informa al público con la historia de los producto, sus atributos o por que se diferencian, e incluso se asesora a los proveedores en la definición del precio apropiado para sus piezas, con el propósito de reconocer la labor de los emprendedores y comercializar sus creaciones con un precio justo. 
En el 2011, nuestro espacio comenzó con 60 mts2 y 12 diseñadores emergentes, actualmente reúne piezas de vestuario, joyería, complementos, objetos y mobiliario de hogar en una exhibición de 300 mts cuadrados, albergando piezas de 80 diseñadores. 
"Nos interesa que nuestros visitantes aprendan, entiendan y aprecien el talento emergente para que puedan conocer historias, experiencias y productos que los acompañen por siempre".
Para conectar al diseñador y al consumidor final lo ideal es informar al público con la historia de cada producto, e incluso generar un relacionamiento mas cercano con los proveedores para crecer juntos y que los productos ofrecidos cumplan las expectativas de quienes nos visitan.
Para que un diseñador emergente pueda exhibir sus creaciones en Makeno, es necesario un proceso de curaduría, que implica revisar asuntos como la coherencia entre la marca y la filosofía de la tienda, la historia del producto, sus propósitos y la usabilidad.

Cueros Vélez is a Colombian manufacturer and retailer of leather goods, established in 1986 and headquartered in Medellín, Antioquia. The company has become a significant player in the Latin American fashion industry, employing over 4,000 direct employees and creating an additional 2,500 indirect jobs. Cueros Vélez operates six production plants with a vertically integrated process that includes everything from raw material handling to delivery.

The company offers a wide range of leather products, including bags, shoes, belts, clothing, travel accessories, wallets, and more, all made from 100% leather. Cueros Vélez has a strong retail presence, with 270 stores across 45 cities in Colombia and approximately 30 international locations in five Latin American countries. The company also collaborates with 120 suppliers to maintain a robust supply chain and invests in employee development through its internal training program, "Velez University." Cueros Vélez generates around $422.2 million in revenue, reflecting its strong market position.

Un espacio para todos, abiertos a toda clase de emprendimiento y de consumidor.
La moda no discrimina, y la belleza esta en la diversidad. Queremos ser parte de este cambio, aún sabiendo que estamos eligiendo un camino largo y difícil. Confiamos en nuestros valores y esa es nuestra misión.

Short Description:
Wearsos is a social enterprise that transforms retired airline seats from Southwest Airlines® Repurpose with Purpose Program Global Sustainability initiative into high-end bags and accessories, while empowering and educating women in the rural community of Turrialba, Costa Rica. Our name, Wearsos, symbolizes the fusion of "wear" and "sostenibilidad," the Spanish word for sustainability, embodying our core mission.

Wearsos is guided by three key principles: 

Waste Reduction: We're conscious of fashion's environmental impact and take strides to minimize it. By repurposing retired airline seat leather, we divert tons of material from landfills, promoting a circular economy.

Rural Economic Activation: In Turrialba, Costa Rica, we provide meaningful employment to women artisans, catalyzing economic growth and community empowerment through fair work opportunities.

Promoting Educational Spaces: Equipping women with leatherworking and entrepreneurship skills, we're enhancing individual lives and supporting educational initiatives that foster innovation and self-sufficiency.

MONOIC is a Colombian based ready-to-wear clothing brand born in 2021, that seeks to be the convergence between contemporary and high-end streetwear through superior quality in the design and materials of our products. 

Since our launch, we have been seeking to create garments under the core value of producing them with the highest quality standards in fabric, fit and manufacturing processes. Every detail counts, and as simple as it may seem, it makes the difference. Each of our garments are designed between Colombia and New York and produced in Colombia, L.A and Europe.

At MONOIC, quality prevails over quantity, this being our core concept, which is respected in every action we make. All our collections are launched with limited units to ensure that the products that our consumers receive are exclusive. Moreover, we truly believe that to tackle sustainability in the fashion industry our garments must last, not just physically but in their design and style. In MONOIC we focus in using the best production techniques, high quality fabrics and designing timeless garments that will last over time without losing their essence.

MONOIC was born from a dream, a passion and a vision. That's why we believe, visualize and work for our dreams and aspirations. No matter how long the path is, it must be followed and in MONOIC that is the foundation of our creation. 

MONOIC a brand inspired by dreams, created for dreamers. Hign-end streetwear for everyday use.

Ruth Schaffer, diseñadora y titular de Kaira, comenzó su camino en la arquitectura.
La pasión por lo artístico la vinculó con la escultura, continuó su búsqueda expresiva acercándose a la joyería contemporánea y logró expresarse con el lenguaje del metal y toda su potencialidad creativa. Comprometida con el cuidado del medioambiente junto con su trabajo de investigación y experimentación le permitieron desarrollar piezas y colecciones con nuevas materialidades. La naturaleza es una fuente inagotable de inspiración en Kaïra. Muchas de las piezas se encuentran inspiradas en ella y cada línea de la firma representa uno de sus cuatro elementes: Fuego, Tierra, Aire y Agua.

Amara is a nonprofit organization founded in 2006, dedicated to making online video content accessible to everyone, regardless of language or ability. As a project of the Participatory Culture Foundation, Amara leverages technology and community collaboration to foster inclusivity and understanding in the digital space. The organization is powered by a diverse community of volunteers, educators, filmmakers, and language experts who work together to expand access to digital media.

Amara offers a cloud-based platform for creating, editing, and managing captions and subtitles in over 50 languages. Their services include professional captioning and translation, community collaboration on projects, and tools designed to make educational and entertainment videos accessible to people with hearing impairments and those who speak different languages. Amara also plays a role in preserving endangered languages by documenting spoken content through captioning, supporting language revitalization efforts. Through its initiatives, Amara aims to bridge the digital divide and promote a more inclusive world.

Our purpose is to create a sustainable and beneficial ecosystem to those who buy and those who produce. Our objective is to revalue the work done by the folk artist and the traditional Peruvian embroiderer art.

Jira Preloved nace de la convicción de que una ropa, zapatos o libros pueden tener muchas vidas. Sabemos que los papás y mamás siempre quieren ofrecer lo mejor a sus hijos. Pero los pequeños crecen muy rápido y necesitan nuevas prendas. Eso genera un gran costo para las familias. Nosotros decidimos actuar pro-activamente, ayudando a generar un ingreso adicional para las familias que venden sus productos que ya no ocupan y la posibilidad de comprar productos de mucha calidad por un precio más accesible. 

Esta es la economía circular, que genera un ahorro mientras protege a nuestro planeta, pues evita que más residuos necesarios para la producción de nuevos productos sean despejados en el medioambiente.

La marca Jira es el nombre de nuestra hermosa jirafita, pero también creemos que la ropa de tu hijo ya quiere salir de gira y estrenar en otra família!

¡Juntos estamos construyendo un mundo mejor para nuestros hijos!

Short Description:
Empresa nacida en el sur de Chile, inspirada en los paisajes, montañas, océanos, flora y fauna del sur
de Chile (el concepto llegó gracias al apego que han tenido los 3 socios con la naturaleza). 

Certificada
como B pendiente (https://www.sistemab.org/sobre/), que se dedica a la elaboración y diseño de
prendas y accesorios de vestir oversize/streetwear, con un gran seguimiento a tendencias actuales,
combinado con un toque de elegancia. Uno de sus propósitos es educar a la gente y crear un impacto
dentro del consumo humano y de su propia psicología sobre la huella generada por la compra de
productos dañinos para el medio ambiente y la sociedad . 

Todo producto es elaborado a través de
materiales orgánicos como lo es el algodón orgánico, cáñamo o bambú, también de material reciclado
como lo es el PET. Con intenciones de ser parte de un proceso más circular e implementar una nueva
actividad la cuál es el reciclaje textil.
Nos diferenciamos en gran parte por nuestro gran apego a un modelo de negocio sostenible,
desarrollado en gran parte por la economía circular. 

Al igual con nuestros diseños únicos de la marca,
y tallas elaboradas. Nos distinguimos por hacer ropa holgada, elegante y minimalista, con un sello
verde. Actualmente trabajamos a la par con la fundación REFORESTEMOS CHILE en la cuál se
destina el 1% de la venta de cada producto a la reforestación de bosques nativos en el sur de Chile.


Otra iniciativa en la cual hemos estado involucrado, es en clases de lengua RapaNui para hacer un
primer acercamiento a un impacto local, en donde se trabajó con el profesor Allan y Javier, llegando a
abrir un abanico de posibilidades de colaboración con la Isla de RapaNui como sponsor a deportistas
de esa zona como lo es en el surf,o actividades con más impacto en la vida de la gente en la Isla o en
la polinesia, también con esquiadores del sur de Chile Gaspar-Benjamín-Rafael, también músicos
actuales dentro de Chile como Vixor.

En Hermia Diseño y Costura, creamos prendas que trascienden el tiempo, combinando artesanía, innovación y un enfoque personalizado. Nos especializamos en la fusión de la alta costura con el prêt-à-porter, ofreciendo diseños exclusivos que no solo visten, sino que cuentan historias.

Cada pieza de Hermia es atemporal, concebida con un propósito: reflejar la esencia de quien la lleva. Diseñamos con el cliente en mente, asegurando que cada prenda trascienda las tendencias pasajeras y se convierta en un símbolo de estilo personal y elegancia duradera.

Nuestra esencia:
🔹 Prêt-à-Porter – Combinamos la exclusividad de la alta costura con la versatilidad del prêt-à-porter, creando piezas únicas que se adaptan a cada ocasión.
🔹 Diseños a Medida – Cada prenda es una obra de arte confeccionada con precisión, diseñada para realzar la esencia y personalidad de quien la lleva.
🔹 Tendencias y Vanguardia – Capturamos la evolución de la moda y la reinterpretamos en diseños que trascienden el tiempo.
🔹 Atemporalidad con Propósito – Diseñamos prendas que no siguen modas efímeras, sino que se convierten en piezas esenciales, pensadas exclusivamente para cada cliente.
🔹 Desarrollo de Moldería – Nuestros expertos diseñan patrones con la máxima precisión para garantizar un ajuste impecable y un estilo inigualable.
🔹 Técnicas de Producción Avanzadas – Apostamos por la excelencia en cada detalle, utilizando procesos de confección innovadores para garantizar calidad y exclusividad.

En Hermia, no solo creamos moda: damos vida a tu estilo de forma única y perdurable. Descubre la armonía entre elegancia, confort y distinción.

Short Description:
Somos un medio digital especializado en moda  que nació en enero del 2020 para abordar temas de interés actuales e históricos sobre el mundo del Prêt-à-Porter y Haute Couture. Entendemos a la moda como una herramienta que utilizan las personas para expresar su identidad. Por eso, realizamos un análisis social basándonos en datos históricos sobre todos los fenómenos que giran en torno a esta industria.

Reflexionamos sobre problemáticas como el Fast Fashion, trabajo esclavo, reproducción de estereotipos y poca diversidad en la industria. Sin embargo, creemos que la moda es mucho más que sus aspectos negativos, por lo que también analizamos tendencias, eventos, biografías de íconos fashion y producciones audiovisuales relacionadas a la industria. La moda es una vía clave para definir nuestra identidad, y si bien trae aparejadas problemáticas, no podemos dejar de valorar y resaltar sus aspectos positivos.   
Consideramos que la moda es comunicación y entendemos a la misma como un instrumento de reivindicación social que utilizan las personas para expresar sus ideas y opiniones por medio de su vestimenta. Las diferentes formas de vestir están inspiradas en corrientes de estilo, que con el tiempo se convierten en tendencias. A su vez, las tendencias vuelven a instalarse cada determinado tiempo. Filo Black se para en esa línea temporal para analizar este componente cíclico de la moda y observar repeticiones y mutaciones en cada tendencia. 

Únete a este viaje de historia, tendencias y estilo de la mano de Filo Black: Tu herramienta fashionista de empoderamiento. 🖤🤍
